title: asplenium raoulii var. colensoi
intro: Asplenium raoulii var. colensoi is a rare, endemic fern species native to New Zealand, characterized by its unique, delicate fronds. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as its ability to support respiratory health. In traditional Māori medicine, the plant is prepared in various ways, including as a tea, infusion, or poultice, to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
